nodes.description: Paper-local nodes. Reuse the same node_id when the same concept appears in multiple edges within the same paper.

node_id.description: Unique paper-local node identifier such as n1, n2, n3.

label.description: Concise paper-local concept label. Use a short noun phrase grounded in the title/abstract. Do not bake country or year into the label unless it is essential to the concept itself.

surface_forms.description: Distinct surface forms from the title/abstract that refer to the same paper-local concept.

study_context.description: Optional local study context for how this concept is measured or scoped in this paper. Downstream systems may ignore this object when building a context-free conceptual graph.

unit_of_analysis.description: Units of analysis explicitly linked to this node in this paper. Use [] if not specified.

start_year.description: Explicitly mentioned start years relevant to this node in this paper. Use [] if not specified.

end_year.description: Explicitly mentioned end years relevant to this node in this paper. Use [] if not specified.

countries.description: Explicitly mentioned countries relevant to this node in this paper. Prefer ISO-3166-1 alpha-3 codes where clear; otherwise use the country name string. Use [] if not specified.

context_note.description: Brief free-text context note for scope that does not fit the structured fields, such as 'older workers' or 'rural counties'. Use 'NA' if not needed.

edges.description: Paper-local relations between nodes. Do not create transitive edges unless the paper explicitly states them.

edge_id.description: Unique paper-local edge identifier such as e1, e2, e3.

source_node_id.description: Source node identifier. For undirected edges, use the first-mentioned concept as the storage order.

target_node_id.description: Target node identifier. For undirected edges, use the second-mentioned concept as the storage order.

directionality.description: Whether the relationship is directional in the paper text.

relationship_type.description: Semantic type of relation being asserted.

causal_presentation.description: How the paper presents the relation, based on title/abstract language, not on whether the method actually justifies causality.

edge_role.description: Role the edge plays in the paper's argument.

claim_status.description: What the paper says about the relation.

explicitness.description: How explicitly the title/abstract frames the edge.

condition_or_scope_text.description: Free-text scope or condition on the edge, such as 'among older workers', 'during recessions', or 'in rural counties'. Use 'NA' if not needed.

claim_text.description: Short normalized claim text representing the relation, ideally in a compact 'A -> B' or 'A -- B' style grounded in the paper language.

evidence_text.description: Short supporting excerpt or close paraphrase from the title/abstract only. Use 'NA' only if no concise supporting text can be isolated.

sign.description: Direction of impact if directional and stated.

effect_size.description: Effect size or magnitude if reported in the title/abstract. Use 'NA' if not reported.

statistical_significance.description: Statistical significance status stated in the title/abstract.

evidence_method.description: Method used to support the edge, if stated in the title/abstract. Use the closest listed family. Use 'other' only when a method is clearly stated but does not fit the listed families. Use 'do_not_know' when the abstract does not reveal enough.

evidence_method_other_description.description: Brief description when evidence_method is 'other'. Otherwise use 'NA'.

nature_of_evidence.description: Broad nature of evidence behind the edge.

uses_data.description: True if this edge is supported by data use in the paper as described in the title/abstract; false otherwise.

sources_of_exogenous_variation.description: Sources of exogenous variation used for the edge if explicitly stated. Use 'NA' if not stated.

tentativeness.description: How tentative the paper's language is for this edge.
